feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Print Handling Unit Label During Creation of Inbound Delivery
entry.content: Dear Experts, As of the 2508 release, it is now possible to print HU labels that are created during inbound delivery creation by automatic packing based on packing instructions. A new parameter "Process" should be available in the Output Parameter Determination App. After several tests, I was not able to find such parameter. Do you know how to solve the issue? Thanks!

entry.title: Authorization Request for Sales BOM Access
entry.content:
Dear Team,

As BOM masking functionality is not available in SAP Public Cloud, we request an authorization-based restriction for BOM access.


Please can you outline how to maintain a BOM Role for the BOM Maintenance apps like Maintain Bill of Material to allow only users view / change Sales BOM (BOM Usage 5).

 

  • No access to Production BOM (Usage 1)
  • No access to Engineering/Plant BOM

 

Kindly create a business role/catalog with restriction on BOM Usage = 5 (Sales BOM only).

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Bank Keys
entry.content:
Hi, 

we need to have all rules to define the correct value in "Bank key" field, for each Bank Master Data and for each country. We have to migrate the Bank Master Data and we have to avoid too error.

What is the CBC step where can I find the country rule? On SAP HELP PORTAL, can we find them?

We are waiting for your answer.

Thanks,

A.

entry.title: Copy control for item category HUPB is missing in LF > IV - what to do with open deliveries?
entry.content:
Hi all

In SAP public cloud the copy control for HUPB is missing for IV billing documents originating from a LF delivery document. 

After raising a ticket with SAP for this, they advised me to create a Feature request to have this Copy control installed. But at the same time to also raise a question here on how to deal with the open deliveries. 

Through the app 'Create billing documents' the users create invoices from deliveries. But in the intercompany flow these deliveries remain open as HUPB items cannot be invoiced. And as long as the copy control is not there the list will keep increasing. 

Is there a known workaround we could do to have these deliveries closed, so they do not remain open? Or any other solution to this problem?

Kr

entry.title: Set Field Mandatory for Profitability Segment in Post General Journal Entries
entry.content:
Hi SAP community,

My business needs to set up mandatory for 2 fields Product and Ship-to Party in Profitability Segment of line item for specified G/L accounts in Post General Journal Entries.

According SAP team's advice, I tried to create rule in Manage Substitution and Validation Rules - Journal Entries application.

 

However, there are some issues and and also this rule doesn't meet requirement:

  • This rule can not block totally posting in Post General Journal Entries application: after entering data of header and line items - without entering data of Assign Profitability Segment, I clicked Post, a pop up announces like the screenshot below. And Post button is disabled.

But when I retried to post by click any field and Enter again, Post button is active and I'm able to post normally.

  • The activation of this rule does not achieve the intended requirement of blocking journal entry postings in the Post General Journal Entries app. Moreover, it unexpectedly blocks other business processes, such as create invoice, create sales order.
 
  • Finally, the data range defined for creating the rule does not seem to be correct. For example, for the Ship-to Party, the range is defined as numeric only; however, in practice, all business partners start with an alphabetical character followed by numbers.
 
I'd appreciate it if the experts could review this case and advise whether there is a suitable way to create a rule that fulfills my requirements, or if there is an alternative approach to enforce these two fields as mandatory.
 
Thank you in advance

entry.title: Standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We would like to inquire about the account determination logic for subcontracting goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

1. Background

We are currently using subcontracting processes for both:

  • Finished Goods (FERT)
  • Semi-Finished Goods (HALB)

The business requirement is to separate G/L accounts for:

  • Goods receipt (inventory offset account)
  • Component consumption

We have maintained Automatic Account Determination (GBB) with different:

  • General Modifications (ZOB, VBO)
  • Valuation Classes (e.g., 7900 for HALB, 7920 for FERT)

2. Observations

Based on our testing:

  1. For normal goods receipt (e.g., movement type 501):
    → GBB (ZOB) is applied and G/L accounts can be controlled
  2. For component consumption:
    → GBB (VBO) is applied and G/L accounts can be changed successfully
  3. For subcontracting goods receipt:
    → The inventory offset account is always posted to G/L 50300000
    → Changes in GBB settings (ZOB / VBO) are not reflected
    → Only the consumption part follows VBO settings

3. Questions

We would like to clarify the following:

  1. What is the standard logic used to determine the G/L account for subcontracting goods receipt?
  2. Is the G/L account (e.g., 50300000) determined by:
    • OBYC / GBB configuration, or
    • Other internal logic (e.g., subcontracting or valuation-related logic)?
  3. Is there any configuration (e.g., SSCUI) to control or override this account?
  4. Is this behavior standard in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

4. Expectation

We would like to confirm whether it is possible to:

  • Assign different G/L accounts for subcontracting goods receipt
    (e.g., separate accounts for FERT and HALB)

If not, we would appreciate clarification on:

  • Standard design limitations
  • Recommended alternatives

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
Michi

entry.title: Is there any limit for creation of no. of company codes in Public Cloud
entry.content: If Customer wants to create 1000+ company codes in SAP Public Cloud edition for US Country, are there any limitations or cost implications, that should be considered? Is there any reference KBA Note available?

entry.title: How to increase the sales office description field characters length from 20 to 40 in CBC
entry.content:
Question 1:  

The sales office description field in CBC is limited to 20 characters. Please confirm whether this limitation can be increased.  

Question 2: Bulk creation of sales offices & bulk creation of sales office addresses are required, as approximately 300 sales offices along with their addresses need to be maintained. Please confirm if this is supported. 

Could you please check and let us know the feasibility of this?

entry.title: Excel extraction financial statement from app Balance Sheet/Income Statement
entry.content:
Hello,

we need to extract the financial statement the same level of details as available in the app "Balance Sheet/Income Statement" but in Excel format (not pdf). We tried to download the info from "Balance Sheet/Income Statement - multidimensional" but the output is not readable, at all.

Thanks,

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Revenue Recognition URR - Requested function ENT1 is not available here - Customer Projects
entry.content:
Following the blog by Xin Fang — https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/an-introduction-of-the-end-2-end-process-in-universal-revenue-recognition/ba-p/14333641 —

We are attempting to replicate the scenario of using a Professional Services Customer Project within a Solution Order in order to enable Universal Revenue Recognition (URR).

Our issue arises when, within the project, we attempt to maintain the SSP that should be transferred to the Solution Order. We do not encounter this issue with Sales Orders.

Steps to Reproduced by my team:Create the solution order, we use the app Manage Solution Order: 

1. Create the solution order, we use the app Manage Solution Order:

We add the customer (for this example we are using 24100001) and description:

Then we add the product configured to create a project item (in this case PROJECT) and press enter:

To generate the project, we must change the item status to release and Save:

We will press the Refresh button and in Transaction History we will see the project:

If we press on the name of the project, it will lead us to the Plan Customer Project app, here the first thing we would do is to create a Work Package:

Once the Work Package is created, we change the stage to contract preparation:

We create a billing item, with the following: Contract type --> Fixed price, Product --> URR_FPPRJ_1, assign the work package that we created, Amount to be Billed --> 2000 for example, SSP --> 3000.

Then we click in to the billing item to create the billing due date:

At last, we go back and Save:

Here is where we receive the following error that won’t allow us to save our project:

We appreciate your support and look forward to your response.
Thank you very much.

entry.title: Production Quantity Control – Input vs Output Not Balanced in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am facing an issue related to production quantity control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition).

From a business perspective, during the production process, the total input quantity (raw materials consumed) should be equal to or greater than the total output quantity (finished goods + semi-finished goods + by-products).

Example:

  • Raw material consumed: 10,000 KG
  • Expected total output: ≤ 10,000 KG (distributed between finished goods, semi-finished goods, and by-products)

However, the system currently allows posting production where the total output quantity exceeds the consumed input quantity, without any error or blocking message.

Issue:
There is no system control or validation preventing:

  • Overproduction compared to consumed raw materials
  • Inconsistency between input and output quantities

Questions:

  1. Is there any standard configuration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to enforce this control?
  2. Can the system be configured to block or warn when output quantity exceeds input quantity?
  3. Is this behavior standard, or does it require a custom enhancement (e.g., BAdI or validation logic)?
  4. Are there any recommended best practices to ensure quantity consistency in production orders?

Any guidance or similar experiences would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Delivery getting blocked for credit segment even if the credit segment not assigned to customer
entry.content:
Delivery is blocked at outbound delivery by credit check; credit check log shows messages such as "Credit check executed with rule ZC", "Credit limit 0.00 has been exceeded", "Oldest open item is less than 010 days overdue", and "Check was not successful".


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: No Mass Upload Option for Standard Text App – Possible Workarounds?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working with the Standard Text functionality in SAP (APP: Manage Standard Text) and noticed that there is no standard option available for mass upload of text templates (e.g. via app or standard transaction).

This becomes quite time-consuming when maintaining a large number of text modules manually.

 

My questions:

Is there any standard approach for mass uploading standard texts?
Are there recommended tools, reports, or APIs to handle this?
Has anyone implemented a custom solution (e.g. via LSMW, BAPI, or ABAP program)?

Context:
We need to upload and maintain a large set of recurring text templates efficiently, ideally with a structured import (e.g. from Excel or CSV).

I would appreciate any insights, best practices, or experience you can share.

Thanks in advance!
Best regards

Markus

entry.title: Cost Difference Not Posted Automatically at Subcontracting Goods Receipt (FERT, Standard Cost)
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

We would like to know if the cost difference can be posted at Subcontracting Goods Receipt. Please refer to the content as follows:

(1) System Information:

System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
Business Scenario: Subcontracting process (HW business)
Material Type: FERT (Finished Product) and ROH (Raw Material) and HALB (Semi-Finished Goods)
Price Control: S (Standard Price) for FERT, V (Average Price) for ROH and HALB

(2) Background:
In our subcontracting scenario, we are using standard cost for finished products (FERT).We would like to understand whether the system can automatically capture and post the cost difference between:

Standard cost of the finished product
Actual cost derived from component consumption and subcontracting service

(3) Test Scenario:

We executed the following test:

Finished Product: FERT (Standard Price = 1,500,000 JPY)
Subcontracting Purchase Order: 1 JPY
Components: HALB / ROH consumed as per BOM

Step 1: Goods Receipt (Subcontracting Inbound)
System posted:

Dr Inventory (Finished Product): 1,500,000 JPY
Cr COGS (50300000): 1,500,000 JPY
Dr Subcontracting Service (Dummy): 1 JPY
Cr GR/IR: 1 JPY
Component consumption postings were also generated (HALB / ROH → COGS / consumption accounts)

Step 2: Supplier Invoice (SIV)
System posted:

Dr GR/IR: 1 JPY
Cr Accounts Payable: 1 JPY

Observed Result:

GR/IR and AP postings are correctly generated based on PO amount
Inventory is posted at standard price
Component consumption is posted correctly

However:

No posting is generated to capture the difference between:

Standard cost (1,500,000 JPY)
Actual cost (components + subcontracting service)

(4) Expected Behavior / Question:

We would like to confirm:

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to automatically post the cost difference at the time of Goods Receipt for subcontracting?
> If yes:
What configuration or scope item is required?
Can this be controlled via account determination (e.g., GBB/BSX) or other settings?
> If not:
Is this behavior standard system design?
Is the only supported approach to capture cost differences via Material Ledger / actual costing at period-end?
Are there any alternative standard solutions to capture this variance automatically (e.g., separate posting logic, enhancement, or best practice)?

Additional Note:
We are currently not using Material Ledger / actual costing.

Request:
Please confirm whether this behavior is standard and advise if any standard functionality exists to fulfill this requirement.

Thank you for your support.

Best Regards,

Michi

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Action Box linking Fiori Application
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Quality Notification, and we would like to enable the usage of action box to call the "Manage Complaints Against Supplier".

We have done the following configuration:

1. Defining Follow-Up Actions for Notification Tasks | SAP Help Portal
2. Action Box | SAP Help Portal
3. Follow-Up Actions for Tasks | SAP Help Portal
4. Activated the BADI "FOA_EXECUTION_FOR_TASK", "BADI_QQM_NOTIF_ACTIONBOX_FOA", "FOA_SIMULATION_FOR_TASK" and "FOA_BACKWARD_COMPLIANCE".

We successfully created new item "Complaint Against Supplier" within action box, however, upon clicking this item, no action is triggered. Could you guide us on how to link the "Manage Complaints Against Supplier" Fiori app so that clicking this item will call and launch the application?

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Thank you!

entry.title: Mass Deletion of PRs with Deleted PO Items (S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am looking for a "Clean Core" way to manage Purchase Requisition (PR) hygiene in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
When a PO item is flagged for deletion, the linked PR item reopens as an active requirement. We need an efficient way to mass delete or close.
Looking for standard Cloud-native solutions or best practices.
Thank You in Advance.

Regards,
Prathamesh

entry.title: Automatic payment processing using DME for International payments()S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We would like clarification regarding automatic payment processing using DME for International payments.

We have various suppliers who transact in multiple currencies and bank accounts

  • AUD – AUD bank account
  • USD – USD Bank account
  • NZD – NZD bank account

Each currency corresponds to a different bank account maintained with the supplier master data.

Is it possible in SAP Hana S4 Public cloud to automatically determine and use the correct Vendor bank account during the payment based on Currency?

entry.title: Unpredictable Amount in Lease in contract
entry.content:
Dear expert,

This question is create for solution discussion for Unpredictable Amount in Lease-in Contract.

Business Background:

We have several vendor sending recurring invoice for the lease contract. However, due to inforecast business impact, the invoice from vendor could be different with the invoice generated from Leased-in module (1T6).

Any protentional solution for this. How could we deal with the difference which the total amount of AP should also considered in the asset we transferred (IFRS ledger).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Goods movement not automatically process when cancelling production order confirmation
entry.content:
Dear sap,

When i confirm the production order by CO11N, the related goods movement could be automatically processed at the same time, since i set the backflush & auto GR.

But when i cancel the production order confirmation by CO13, the related goods movement could not be automatically processed at the same time. And the goods movement enter into reprocess goods movements-COGI, and i found the error message CO469. I can only reprocess the goods movement by COGI.

How could i process the goods movement at the same time by CO13 ?

Thanks.

Ying

entry.title: Additional Fields required in Asset master
entry.content:
1. Brand and Model number and Custodian - should be part of General data.
  2. Invoice number should be part of Origin
  3. Building, Floor - under Time Dependent data -> Location

Please let us know how we can accomodate the above mentioned fields in Asset master.

CB user -> CB9980000001 

Regards,

Amit
